explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: The company's 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report describes an annual review and approval process for its Code of Conduct. This report also describes an update to the Code of Conduct, which states revised provisions addressing the protection of company assets and information, including the use of artificial intelligence.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The "Cheniere 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report" provides evidence for the **governance** pillar. This report details an annual review and approval process for the Code of Conduct, which involves specific roles, demonstrating the execution of governance practices.
relevant_sources.title: Cheniere 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report

relevant_sources.summary: Cheniere's 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The report details an update to the company's Code of Conduct, which now includes revised provisions specifically addressing the protection of company assets and information, including the use of artificial intelligence. This demonstrates a formal extension of the company's governance framework to encompass AI technologies.
relevant_sources.title: 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Cheniere Energy's published materials address governance, describing an annual review and approval process for the Code of Conduct in its 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report. This represents 1 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars with documented evidence, specifically at the policy level. Drawing from 11 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
